system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Prescription for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Maithili (code=mai, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Bihar, district=Saharsa.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Maithili.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 18
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Below Primary
- zone: Rural
- state: Bihar
- district: Saharsa
- first_language: Maithili
- clinical_persona_summary: An 18-year-old male from Saharsa, Bihar, currently married, with below primary education and a rural background. He works as a farmhand and speaks Maithili as his first language, with limited Hindi comprehension. His lifestyle includes a habit of humming folk songs while working and occasional attendance at adult-education classes.

Document format guide for Prescription (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|28]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-1001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Rx: <drug dose frequency>  (do NOT tag drug names as entities)

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PATIENT_ID — Patient ID e.g. [[PATIENT_ID|PID-77821]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
नुस्खा — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Saharsa District Hospital]]
मरीज [[PATIENT_NAME|Ram Prasad Yadav]], [[AGE|18]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-2024-0815-001]]
डाक्टर [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Kumar]]
फोन: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9431023456]]
पता: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|Village: Mahua, Post: Saur Bazar, Saharsa, Bihar - 852201]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
मरीज ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-98765]]
जिला: [[DISTRICT|Saharsa]]

मुख्य शिकायत:
मरीजक बाम पैरक निचला भागमे छह माससँ एकटा नहि भरय बला घाव अछि, जाहिमे बीच-बीचमे दर्द आ कखनो-कखनो दुर्गंधयुक्त स्राव होइत अछि। ओ कहैत छथि जे पहिने सेहो एहि तरहक समस्या भेल छल, जाहिमे ऊपरसँ एंटीबायोटिक लगा कऽ उपचार कयल गेल छल मुदा कोनो सुधार नहि भेल। मरीजक शरीरमे कमजोरी, दू मासमे लगभग 5 किलो वजन कम भऽ जानाय आ साँझमे हल्का बुखार अबैत अछि।

नैदानिक निष्कर्ष:
जाँच कयला पर, मरीज सचेत छथि, सभ किछु बुझि रहल छथि आ कमजोर देखाइत छथि। महत्वपूर्ण संकेत स्थिर अछि: BP 110/70 mmHg, Pulse 88/min, Temperature 99.4°F। बाम पैरक भीतरी भागमे 8x6 cm क एकटा पैघ, अनियमित, मृत घाव अछि, जाहिमे किनारा ढीला अछि आ दुर्गंध अछि। आसपासक चमड़ी लाल अछि। पैरक नाड़ी (pedal pulses) महसूस भऽ रहल अछि मुदा कम अछि। कोनो खास लिम्फ नोड (lymphadenopathy) नहि भेटल अछि। प्रयोगशाला जाँचसँ हल्का नॉर्मोसाइटिक एनीमिया (Hb 9.8 g/dL) आ बढ़ल ESR (85 mm/hr) क पता चलल अछि। एकटा अस्थायी निदान कयल गेल अछि जे ई एकटा पुरान, नहि भरय बला पैरक घाव अछि, जे संभवतः कोनो पुरान संक्रमण या कैंसरक कारण भऽ सकैत अछि। मरीजक उम्र, स्थिति आ जोखिमक कारककेँ देखैत, स्क्वैमस सेल कार्सिनोमा (squamous cell carcinoma) सन कैंसरकेँ नकारल नहि जा सकैत अछि।

योजना:
1.  आगू जाँच: कैंसरकेँ नकारबाक लेल घावक किनाराक एकटा पंच बायोप्सी (punch biopsy) हिस्टोपैथोलॉजिकल जाँचक लेल भेजल गेल अछि। ब्लड शुगर लेवल आ सीरम प्रोटीन इलेक्ट्रोफोरेसिसक आदेश सेहो देल गेल अछि।
2.  घावक देखभाल: घावकेँ प्रतिदिन सामान्य सलाइनसँ साफ कयल जायत आ एकटा नॉन-एडहेरेंट ड्रेसिंगसँ ढकल जायत। ऊपरसँ एंटीमाइक्रोबियल मरहम लगाओल जायत।
3.  पोषण सहायता: मरीजकेँ अपन प्रोटीन आ कैलोरीक सेवन बढ़यबाक सलाह देल जाइत अछि। एकटा पोषण विशेषज्ञक पास रेफरल कयल गेल अछि।
4.  फॉलो-अप: बायोप्सी रिपोर्टक समीक्षा करबाक आ आगू क प्रबंधनक योजना बनाबय लेल एक सप्ताहमे फॉलो-अप अपॉइंटमेंट निर्धारित कयल गेल अछि।
5.  दवा:
    - टैबलेट Metronidazole 400 mg, एकटा टैबलेट दिनमे दू बेर 7 दिन धरि।
    - टैबलेट Paracetamol 500 mg, बुखार या दर्दक लेल आवश्यकतानुसार एकटा टैबलेट।
    - टैबलेट Azithromycin 500 mg, एकटा टैबलेट प्रतिदिन 5 दिन धरि।
    - मल्टीविटामिन टैबलेट, एकटा टैबलेट प्रतिदिन।

कृपया निर्धारित दवाइयक निर्देशानुसार सेवन करू आ घावक नीक स्वच्छता बना कऽ राखू। फॉलो-अप अपॉइंटमेंटक लेल वापस आउ।
generated_text_en:
Prescription — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Saharsa District Hospital]]
Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Ram Prasad Yadav]], [[AGE|18]] / [[GENDER|Male]], MRN [[MRN|RX-2024-0815-001]]
Dr. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Sanjay Kumar]]
Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9431023456]]
Address: [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|Village: Mahua, Post: Saur Bazar, Saharsa, Bihar - 852201]]
ABHA ID: [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
Patient ID: [[PATIENT_ID|PID-98765]]
District: [[DISTRICT|Saharsa]]

Chief Complaint:
The patient presents with a persistent, non-healing ulcer on the left lower leg for the past six months, associated with intermittent pain and occasional foul-smelling discharge. He reports a history of similar episodes treated with topical antibiotics without resolution. The patient also complains of generalized weakness, weight loss of approximately 5 kg over two months, and low-grade evening fevers.

Clinical Findings:
On examination, the patient is conscious, oriented, and appears cachectic. Vital signs are stable: BP 110/70 mmHg, Pulse 88/min, Temperature 99.4°F. A large, irregular, necrotic ulcer measuring 8x6 cm is present on the medial aspect of the left leg, with undermined edges and a foul odor. The surrounding skin is erythematous. Pedal pulses are palpable but diminished. No significant lymphadenopathy is noted. Laboratory investigations reveal mild normocytic anemia (Hb 9.8 g/dL) and elevated ESR (85 mm/hr). A provisional diagnosis of a chronic, non-healing leg ulcer, likely secondary to a chronic infection or malignancy, is made. Given the patient's age, presentation, and risk factors, a malignancy such as squamous cell carcinoma cannot be ruled out.

Plan:
1.  Further investigations: A punch biopsy of the ulcer margins has been sent for histopathological examination to rule out malignancy. Blood sugar levels and a serum protein electrophoresis have also been ordered.
2.  Wound care: The wound is to be cleaned daily with normal saline and covered with a non-adherent dressing. An application of topical antimicrobial ointment will be provided.
3.  Nutritional support: The patient is advised to increase his protein and caloric intake. A referral to a nutritionist is made.
4.  Follow-up: The patient is scheduled for a follow-up appointment in one week to review the biopsy report and plan further management.
5.  Medications:
    -   Tablet Metronidazole 400 mg, one tablet twice daily for 7 days.
    -   Tablet Paracetamol 500 mg, one tablet as needed for fever or pain.
    -   Tablet Azithromycin 500 mg, one tablet once daily for 5 days.
    -   Multivitamin tablet, one tablet daily.

Please take the prescribed medications as directed and maintain good wound hygiene. Return for the follow-up appointment.
judge_reasoning: Maithili Devanagari prose matches expected language; all entity TYPEs allowed; age/sex/clinical content plausible for chronic ulcer workup; no script or length issues.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Malayalam (code=ml, script=Malayalam). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Kerala, district=Kozhikode.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Malayalam for Malayalam.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 18
- marital_status: Never Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Higher Secondary/Intermediate Pre-University/Senior Secondary
- zone: Rural
- state: Kerala
- district: Kozhikode
- first_language: Malayalam
- clinical_persona_summary: A 18-year-old female from rural Kozhikode, Kerala, who is never married and has no formal occupation, completed higher secondary education and is fluent in Malayalam with functional English. She is a homemaker with a background in traditional cooking and community-based nutrition outreach, and her lifestyle includes flexible routines, community harmony, and occasional yoga. Her cultural context is rooted in the Malabar coast's customs, with a diet rich in coconut, tamarind, and traditional sadhya dishes.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]

  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Marathi (code=mr, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Maharashtra, district=Chandrapur.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Marathi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 65
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Primary
- zone: Urban
- state: Maharashtra
- district: Chandrapur
- first_language: Marathi
- clinical_persona_summary: A 65-year-old male, currently married, retired with primary education, living in an urban area of Maharashtra. His first language is Marathi, with functional Hindi proficiency and limited English. He maintains a lifestyle involving mindful meditation, light yoga, and community volunteering, with occasional visits to nearby hill stations.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

persona: Ankur Gain, a 54-year-old inventory veteran with a competitive edge, they blend meticulous stock-keeping with a love for Himalayan art, folk music, and trekking, while habitually juggling cash savings and occasional health neglect.
cultural_background: Ankur was born and raised in the bustling hill town of Darjeeling, West Bengal, where the Nepali‑speaking Gorkha community forms a core part of the social fabric. Living and working in Darjeeling has given him conversational ability in Hindi, primarily for interacting with customers and colleagues, though he does not consider himself fully proficient. He has little exposure to Bengali despite residing in West Bengal, and he does not have any additional foreign language skills.
skills_and_expertise: Ankur has over three decades of experience as a stock clerk, giving him strong expertise in inventory control, stock auditing, and supply‑chain basics. He is adept at using manual ledgers as well as computer‑based inventory software, performs accurate data entry, and maintains meticulous records. His role requires effective customer interaction, product knowledge, and basic accounting for stock reconciliation. He possesses good organizational abilities, time‑management, and team coordination skills, and he can handle high‑pressure situations in busy retail environments. His curiosity about art also equips him with a basic understanding of local handicrafts and cultural products, which helps in presenting items attractively.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Nepali (code=ne, script=Devanagari). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=West Bengal, district=Darjiling.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Devanagari for Nepali.

PLACEMENT (OPD header): First line MUST include BOTH [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] (facility name) AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] (facility code) — never substitute one for the other.
Put [[EMPLOYEE_ID|…]] on the registrar/clerk line and [[RELATIVE_NAME|…]] on the attendant/relative line. Include [[DOB|YYYY-MM-DD]] and [[OCCUPATION|…]] with demographics.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 54
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Stock Clerks, Other
- education_level: Graduate & above
- zone: Urban
- state: West Bengal
- district: Darjiling
- first_language: Nepali
- clinical_persona_summary: A 54-year-old male stock clerk, currently married, with a graduate education working in an urban West Bengal district. Nepali is his first language, and he resides in Darjiling. His lifestyle includes a habit of cash savings and occasional health neglect.

Document format guide for OPD Slip (Outpatient Department) (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (adapt language/script; keep tags):
OPD Slip | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital X]] | ID [[HOSPITAL_ID|DH-X-01]]
Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] | DOB [[DOB|1992-03-14]] | Age: [[AGE|34]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Female]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. ...]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|...]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4412]] | District: [[DISTRICT|...]]
Chief complaint: <short clinical line matching assigned domain>

MANDATORY inline entities (13 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- OCCUPATION — Occupation e.g. [[OCCUPATION|Teacher]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- EMPLOYEE_ID — Employee ID e.g. [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ओपिडी स्लिप | [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Darjiling District Hospital]] | आइडी [[HOSPITAL_ID|DDH-WB-001]]
बिरामी: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kumar Sharma]] | जन्ममिति [[DOB|1970-05-22]] | उमेर: [[AGE|54]] | लिङ्ग: [[GENDER|Male]]
व्यवसाय: [[OCCUPATION|Stock Clerk]] | एमआरएन: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | डाक्टर: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anirban Chatterjee]]
सम्बन्धित: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sunita Sharma]] | फोन: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9832145678]]
दर्ता कर्मचारी आइडी: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-2024-014]] | जिल्ला: [[DISTRICT|Darjiling]]
मुख्य गुनासो: बिरामीको विगत तिन दिनदेखि दाहिने इन्गुइनल क्षेत्रमा दुखाइ र सुन्निने समस्या छ, जसमा यस्तै प्रकृतिका घटनाहरूको इतिहास छ।
परीक्षण: दाहिने इन्गुइनल क्षेत्रमा लगभग 5x4 सेन्टिमिटरको, थिच्दा खुल्ने (reducible), दुख्ने गाँठो देखिन्छ, जुन नधड्किने (non-pulsatile) छ र छालामा कुनै परिवर्तन छैन। खोकी लगाउँदा कुनै धक्का महसुस भएन। आन्द्राको आवाज सबै चतुर्थांशमा उपस्थित छ।
निदान: दाहिने इन्गुइनल हर्निया, थिच्दा खुल्ने।
योजना: 1. हर्निया र शल्यक्रियाको आवश्यकताका बारेमा बिरामीलाई परामर्श दिने। 2. ऐच्छिक दाहिने इन्गुइनल हर्नियोराफीको तालिका बनाउने। 3. दुखाइ कम गर्ने औषधि (analgesics) सिफारिस गर्ने र भारी उठाउनबाट बच्न सल्लाह दिने। 4. एक हप्तामा शल्यक्रिया ओपिडीमा फलो-अप गर्ने।

Chief complaint: Patient presents with a painful, swollen right inguinal region for the past three days, with a history of similar episodes.
Examination: Right inguinal area shows a reducible, tender mass approximately 5x4 cm, non-pulsatile, with no overlying skin changes. No cough impulse noted. Bowel sounds are present in all quadrants.
Diagnosis: Right inguinal hernia, reducible.
Plan: 1. Counsel patient regarding hernia and indications for surgery. 2. Schedule for elective right inguinal herniorrhaphy. 3. Prescribe analgesics and advise to avoid heavy lifting. 4. Follow-up in surgical OPD in one week.

professional_persona: Kishor Maharana is a 35‑year‑old master craftsman specializing in hand‑crafted footwear, blending traditional leather stitching with modest design tweaks, and they manage their workshop with diligent bookkeeping, reliable customer service, and a practical curiosity that drives them to explore new tools and expand into nearby towns and online platforms.
linguistic_persona: Kishor Maharana primarily communicates in Odia, comfortably reading and writing in it, and they possess functional Hindi for official dealings while their exposure to English is limited to trade‑specific jargon and a few borrowed terms.
sports_persona: Kishor Maharana follows cricket passionately on the radio, roots for Chennai Super Kings in the IPL, and joins occasional village‑level cricket matches and kabaddi games, using them as a lively outlet for fitness and camaraderie.
arts_persona: Kishor Maharana revels in Odia folk songs and bhajans, often tapping the dholak to tunes by Akshaya Mohanty and Prafulla Kar, while also enjoying Bollywood blockbusters starring Amitabh Bachchan and Shah Rukh Khan, and they occasionally attend Odissi dance showcases during village festivals.
travel_persona: Kishor Maharana prefers budget bus trips to nearby Puri for Rath Yatra and Bhubaneswar for market deals, occasionally journeys to Kolkata by train, and dreams of a pilgrimage to Delhi’s historic forts, planning each outing through word‑of‑mouth and modest mobile apps.
culinary_persona: Kishor Maharana enjoys cooking traditional Odia fare like dalma, chakuli pitha, and roti, seasoning with mustard seeds, ginger and coconut, and they love serving sweet treats such as chhena poda and rasgulla to family and neighbours during festivals.
persona: Kishor Maharana is a practical yet curious village shoemaker who balances tradition with modest innovation, values community harmony above all, and quietly juggles workshop duties, family hopes, and a habit of lingering over paperwork.
cultural_background: Born and raised in the rural village of Nabarangapur, Odisha, Kishor Maharana hails from a traditional Odia artisan community where hand-crafted footwear has been passed down through generations. His cultural life revolves around village festivals like Rath Yatra, Nuakhai and local worship of village deities, as well as communal storytelling and folk music. While deeply rooted in these customs, his innate curiosity leads him to experiment with modest design innovations, reflecting a practical blend of tradition and modernity.
linguistic_background: Kishor’s first language is Odia, which he speaks, reads and writes with confidence, allowing him to manage day‑to‑day transactions, follow local news and keep records for his workshop. He has a basic functional understanding of Hindi, mainly for official communication and occasional market dealings, but he does not read or write Hindi fluently. His schooling only went up to primary level, so his exposure to English is minimal, limited to a few borrowed terms used in the trade.
skills_and_expertise: Kishor is skilled in hand‑crafted shoe making, including leather cutting, pattern drafting, hand stitching, and finishing. He can design simple styles that suit local preferences and repair worn footwear efficiently. His practical mindset ensures consistent quality, while his curiosity pushes him to experiment with modest design tweaks. He also manages the day‑to‑day operations of his small workshop, handling inventory, basic bookkeeping, and direct customer service with a cooperative and reliable approach.
skills_and_expertise_list: ['hand-crafted shoe making', 'leather cutting', 'pattern drafting', 'hand stitching', 'shoe repair', 'basic workshop management', 'inventory tracking', 'customer service']
hobbies_and_interests: In his free time, Kishor enjoys listening to Odia folk songs and bhajans, occasionally playing the dholak during village gatherings. He follows cricket matches on the radio and likes to watch short tutorial videos on shoe design on his mobile phone. Gardening, especially growing seasonal vegetables, offers him quiet relaxation, while cooking traditional Odia dishes such as dalma and roti lets him share meals with family and neighbours. He also participates in local fairs and helps organize community events.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ['listening to Odia folk music', 'playing dholak', 'watching cricket', 'viewing shoe design videos', 'gardening', 'cooking traditional Odia dishes', 'participating in village fairs', 'helping organize community events']
career_goals_and_ambitions: Kishor aims to grow his modest shoe‑making workshop into a more organized small enterprise that can supply nearby towns and potentially tap online platforms. He plans to learn basic modern tailoring tools, improve design variety, and train a couple of apprentices from his village. By increasing his income, he hopes to provide higher‑level education for his children and secure a comfortable retirement for himself, all while preserving the traditional craft and maintaining the harmonious village life he values.

He is married, speaks Odia as his first language, and resides in a rural setting. His lifestyle includes managing workshop paperwork and engaging in community activities.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

Patient: [[PATIENT_NAME|Kishor Maharana]] | DOB [[DOB|1989-07-22]] | Age: [[AGE|35]] | Gender: [[GENDER|Male]]
Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Shoe Maker and Related Workers, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Patnaik]]

Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4021]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Nabarangapur]]
Chief complaint: ରୋଗୀ ତିନି ମାସ ଧରି ଲଗାତାର କମ୍ ମନୋଭାବ, ଆନନ୍ଦହୀନତା ଏବଂ ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ବିଷୟରେ କହୁଛନ୍ତି। ସେ କାମ ଏବଂ ଘରେ ଅଧିକ ବିରକ୍ତି ଅନୁଭବ କରୁଥିବା ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି।

History of present illness: ଶ୍ରୀ କିଶୋର ମହାରଣା, ଜଣେ 35 ବର୍ଷର ପୁରୁଷ ମୋଚି, ଗତ ତିନି ମାସ ମଧ୍ୟରେ ଅବସାଦ ଲକ୍ଷଣର ଧୀରେ ଧୀରେ ଆରମ୍ଭ ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଏକ ବ୍ୟାପକ କମ୍ ମନୋଭାବ, ନିଜର ସାଧାରଣ କାମ ଏବଂ ସାମାଜିକ କାର୍ଯ୍ୟକଳାପରେ ଆଗ୍ରହ ହରାଇବା ଏବଂ ନିଜକୁ ଅଦରକାରୀ ମନେ କରିବା ବିଷୟରେ ବର୍ଣ୍ଣନା କରିଛନ୍ତି। ଶୋଇବା ବାଧାପ୍ରାପ୍ତ ହୋଇଛି, ଶୀଘ୍ର ଶୋଇବା ଉଠିବା ଏବଂ ପୁଣି ଶୋଇବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। ଭୋକ କମିଯାଇଛି ଏବଂ ସେ ପ୍ରାୟ 5 କିଲୋଗ୍ରାମ ଓଜନ ହରାଇଛନ୍ତି। ସେ ଅଧିକ ବିରକ୍ତି ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି, ଯାହା ଫଳରେ ତାଙ୍କ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କ ସହ ଯୁକ୍ତିତର୍କ ହେଉଛି ଏବଂ ତାଙ୍କ କର୍ମଶାଳାର ହିସାବ ରଖିବାରେ ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। କୌଣସି ମାନସିକ ଲକ୍ଷଣ, ଆତ୍ମହତ୍ୟା ଚିନ୍ତା କିମ୍ବା ନିଜକୁ କ୍ଷତି ପହଞ୍ଚାଇବା ବିଷୟରେ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ।

Past psychiatric history: ପୂର୍ବରୁ କୌଣସି ମାନସିକ ରୋଗର ନିଦାନ କିମ୍ବା ଚିକିତ୍ସା ହୋଇନାହିଁ।

Past medical history: କୌଣସି ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱପୂର୍ଣ୍ଣ ଚିକିତ୍ସା ରୋଗ ନାହିଁ।

Medications: କିଛି ନାହିଁ।

Family history: ମା'ଙ୍କ ବିଷୟରେ କୁହାଯାଇଛି ଯେ ତାଙ୍କର ଉଚ୍ଚ ରକ୍ତଚାପ ଅଛି। ମାନସିକ ରୋଗର କୌଣସି ଜଣାଶୁଣା ପାରିବାରିକ ଇତିହାସ ନାହିଁ।

Social history: ବିବାହିତ, ନିଜ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀ ଏବଂ ଦୁଇ ସନ୍ତାନଙ୍କ ସହ ଏକ ଗ୍ରାମାଞ୍ଚଳରେ ରହୁଛନ୍ତି। ଜଣେ ମୋଚି ଭାବରେ କାମ କରନ୍ତି, ଏକ ଛୋଟ କର୍ମଶାଳା ପରିଚାଳନା କରନ୍ତି। ତାଙ୍କ ସ୍ତ୍ରୀଙ୍କଠାରୁ ଭଲ ସାମାଜିକ ସମର୍ଥନ ବିଷୟରେ ଜଣାଇଛନ୍ତି କିନ୍ତୁ ତାଙ୍କ ମନୋଭାବ ଯୋଗୁଁ ସାମ୍ପ୍ରତିକ ଚାପ ବିଷୟରେ ଉଲ୍ଲେଖ କରିଛନ୍ତି। ତମାଖୁ କିମ୍ବା ମଦ୍ୟପାନ ବ୍ୟବହାରକୁ ମନା କରିଛନ୍ତି।

Examination:
Mental state examination: ରୋଗୀ ଭଲ ପୋଷାକ ପିନ୍ଧିଛନ୍ତି କିନ୍ତୁ କ୍ଳାନ୍ତ ଦେଖାଯାଉଛନ୍ତି। କଥାବାର୍ତ୍ତା ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ଗତିରେ ଏବଂ ପରିମାଣରେ ହେଉଛି, ବେଳେବେଳେ ବିରତି ସହିତ। ମନୋଭାବ "କମ୍" ଏବଂ "ଅବସାଦଗ୍ରସ୍ତ" ଭାବରେ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇଛି। ଭାବନା ସୀମିତ ଅଛି। ଚିନ୍ତା ପ୍ରକ୍ରିୟା ଯୁକ୍ତିଯୁକ୍ତ ଏବଂ ସୁସଙ୍ଗତ। କୌଣସି ଭ୍ରମ କିମ୍ବା ହାଲୁସିନେସନ୍ ରିପୋର୍ଟ କରାଯାଇ ନାହିଁ। ଅନ୍ତର୍ଦୃଷ୍ଟି ଠିକ୍ ଅଛି, ନିଜ ଅବସ୍ଥାର ଗୁରୁତରତାକୁ ଗ୍ରହଣ କରିବାରେ କିଛି ଅସୁବିଧା ହେଉଛି। ବିରକ୍ତି ସମ୍ବନ୍ଧରେ ବିଚାର ଶକ୍ତି ବାଧାପ୍ରାପ୍ତ ହୋଇଛି।

Physical examination: ସ୍ୱାଭାବିକ ସୀମା ମଧ୍ୟରେ।

Diagnosis:
1. Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Moderate (F32.1)
2. Insomnia (G47.00)

Treatment plan:
1. Sertraline 50 mg ଦିନକୁ ଥରେ ଆରମ୍ଭ କରନ୍ତୁ, ଏକ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ସହନଶୀଳତା ଅନୁଯାୟୀ 100 mg କୁ ବୃଦ୍ଧି କରନ୍ତୁ।
2. ଅବସାଦ, ଏହାର ଚିକିତ୍ସା ଏବଂ ଅନୁପାଳନର ଗୁରୁତ୍ୱ ବିଷୟରେ ମନୋ-ଶିକ୍ଷା ପ୍ରଦାନ କରନ୍ତୁ।
3. ଶୋଇବା ସ୍ୱଚ୍ଛତା ଅଭ୍ୟାସ ବିଷୟରେ ପରାମର୍ଶ ଦିଅନ୍ତୁ।
4. ଚିକିତ୍ସାର ପ୍ରତିକ୍ରିୟା ଏବଂ ପାର୍ଶ୍ୱ ପ୍ରତିକ୍ରିୟା ଆକଳନ କରିବା ପାଇଁ ଦୁଇ ସପ୍ତାହ ପରେ ଏକ ଫଲୋ-ଅପ୍ ଆପଏଣ୍ଟମେଣ୍ଟ୍ ସ୍ଥିର କରନ୍ତୁ।
5. ସହାୟକ ମନୋଚିକିତ୍ସା ପାଇଁ କାଉନସେଲିଂ ସେଣ୍ଟରକୁ ପଠାନ୍ତୁ।

Occupation: [[OCCUPATION|Shoe Maker and Related Workers, Other]] | MRN: [[MRN|OPD-2024-0815-001]] | Doctor: [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. S. Patnaik]]
Relative: [[RELATIVE_NAME|Sushila Maharana]] | Phone: [[PHONE_NUMBER|9438901234]]
Registrar EmpID: [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-4021]] | District: [[DISTRICT|Nabarangapur]]
Chief complaint: Patient presents with persistent low mood, anhedonia, and difficulty sleeping for the past three months, reporting increased irritability at work and home.

History of present illness: Mr. Kishor Maharana, a 35-year-old male shoemaker, reports a gradual onset of depressive symptoms over the last three months. He describes a pervasive low mood, loss of interest in his usual craft and community activities, and feelings of worthlessness. Sleep is disturbed with early morning awakening and difficulty returning to sleep. Appetite is reduced, and he has lost approximately 5 kg in weight. He reports increased irritability, leading to arguments with his spouse, and difficulty concentrating on his workshop accounts. There are no reported psychotic symptoms, suicidal ideation, or self-harm.

Past psychiatric history: No prior psychiatric diagnoses or treatment.

Past medical history: No significant medical illnesses.

Medications: None.

Family history: Mother is reportedly hypertensive. No known family history of psychiatric illness.

Social history: Married, lives in a rural village with his wife and two children. Works as a shoemaker, managing a small workshop. Reports good social support from his wife but notes recent strain due to his mood. Denies tobacco or alcohol use.

Examination:
Mental state examination: Patient is well-dressed but appears fatigued. Speech is of normal rate and volume, with occasional pauses. Mood is reported as "low" and "depressed." Affect is constricted. Thought process is logical and coherent. No delusions or hallucinations reported. Insight is fair, with some difficulty in accepting the severity of his condition. Judgment is impaired regarding his irritability.

Physical examination: Within normal limits.

Diagnosis:
1. Major Depressive Disorder, Single Episode, Moderate (F32.1)
2. Insomnia (G47.00)

Treatment plan:
1. Initiate antidepressant therapy with Sertraline 50 mg once daily, to be increased to 100 mg after one week as tolerated.
2. Provide psychoeducation regarding depression, its treatment, and the importance of adherence.
3. Advise on sleep hygiene practices.
4. Schedule a follow-up appointment in two weeks to assess response to treatment and side effects.
5. Refer to the counseling center for supportive psychotherapy.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; clinical story fits that persona.

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs; dates/money in plain prose; valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Radiology / Imaging Report for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Punjabi (code=pa, script=Gurmukhi). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Punjab, district=Amritsar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Gurmukhi for Punjabi.

PLACEMENT: Include [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] as the reporting radiologist (e.g. Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]]) — do not leave the doctor name as untagged prose.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 40
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Punjab
- district: Amritsar
- first_language: Punjabi
- clinical_persona_summary: Gurmel Rani is a 40-year-old female, currently married, residing in urban Amritsar, Punjab, with a matriculation education. She is a homemaker with functional English literacy, fluent in Punjabi and Hindi. Her lifestyle includes occasional yoga for stress management and a diet rich in traditional Punjabi cuisine.

Document format guide for Radiology / Imaging Report (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Imaging report — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|...]] [[AGE|45]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|...]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|...]] | Reported by Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Findings: <radiology prose, untagged>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- ENCOUNTER_ID — Encounter / Visit ID e.g. [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- HOSPITAL_ID — Hospital ID e.g. [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
- ABHA_ID — ABHA ID e.g. [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ਰੇਡੀਓਲੋਜੀ ਰਿਪੋਰਟ — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Guru Nanak Dev Hospital]] | [[PATIENT_NAME|Gurmel Rani]] [[AGE|40]] [[GENDER|Female]]
MRN [[MRN|MRN-PUN-2024-001]] Encounter [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-PUN-2024-0815-001]] | ਰਿਪੋਰਟ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਡਾ. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Harpreet Singh]] ਦੁਆਰਾ
ਨਤੀਜੇ: ਮਰੀਜ਼ ਨੇ ਲਗਾਤਾਰ ਸੱਜੇ ਪਾਸੇ ਛਾਤੀ ਵਿੱਚ ਦਰਦ ਅਤੇ ਮਿਹਨਤ ਕਰਨ 'ਤੇ ਹਲਕੀ ਸਾਹ ਦੀ ਤਕਲੀਫ ਦੀ ਮੁੱਖ ਸ਼ਿਕਾਇਤ ਨਾਲ ਪੇਸ਼ ਕੀਤਾ। ਇੱਕ ਪੋਸਟਰੋਐਂਟੀਰੀਅਰ (PA) ਛਾਤੀ ਦਾ ਰੇਡੀਓਗ੍ਰਾਫ ਕੀਤਾ ਗਿਆ ਸੀ। ਫੇਫੜੇ ਦੋਵਾਂ ਪਾਸਿਆਂ ਤੋਂ ਸਾਫ਼ ਹਨ, ਬਿਨਾਂ ਕਿਸੇ ਕੰਸੋਲੀਡੇਸ਼ਨ, ਇਫਿਊਜ਼ਨ, ਜਾਂ ਨਿਊਮੋਥੋਰੈਕਸ ਦੇ। ਦਿਲ ਦਾ ਆਕਾਰ ਅਤੇ ਮੀਡੀਆਸਟਾਈਨਲ ਕੰਟੂਰਸ ਆਮ ਸੀਮਾਵਾਂ ਦੇ ਅੰਦਰ ਹਨ। ਹੱਡੀ ਵਾਲਾ ਥੋਰੈਕਸ, ਜਿਸ ਵਿੱਚ ਪਸਲੀਆਂ ਅਤੇ ਕਲੈਵਿਕਲ ਸ਼ਾਮਲ ਹਨ, ਬਿਨਾਂ ਕਿਸੇ ਖਾਸ ਲੱਛਣ ਦੇ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦਿੰਦਾ ਹੈ। ਕੋਈ ਗੰਭੀਰ ਹੱਡੀ ਦੀਆਂ ਅਸਧਾਰਨਤਾਵਾਂ ਦੀ ਪਛਾਣ ਨਹੀਂ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ ਹੈ। ਡਾਇਆਫ੍ਰਾਮ ਚੰਗੀ ਤਰ੍ਹਾਂ ਦਿਖਾਈ ਦੇ ਰਿਹਾ ਹੈ ਅਤੇ ਕੋਸਟੋਫ੍ਰੇਨਿਕ ਕੋਣ ਬਰਕਰਾਰ ਹਨ। ਨਰਮ ਟਿਸ਼ੂ ਬਿਨਾਂ ਕਿਸੇ ਖਾਸ ਲੱਛਣ ਦੇ ਹਨ। ਪ੍ਰਭਾਵ: PA ਛਾਤੀ ਦੇ ਰੇਡੀਓਗ੍ਰਾਫ 'ਤੇ ਕੋਈ ਗੰਭੀਰ ਕਾਰਡੀਓਪਲਮੋਨਰੀ ਅਸਧਾਰਨਤਾ ਦੀ ਪਛਾਣ ਨਹੀਂ ਕੀਤੀ ਗਈ। ਕਲੀਨਿਕਲ ਸਬੰਧ ਦੀ ਸਲਾਹ ਦਿੱਤੀ ਜਾਂਦੀ ਹੈ।

Her daily life follows a well‑established routine of early morning prayers, Sanskrit study, and household chores, reflecting her preference for familiarity and practicality.
